AMENDED OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STRIKE SLAPP SUIT AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP 128.5 FILED BY PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT LIGIA MELENDEZ' MOTION TO STRIKE SLAPP SUIT AND FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S 23 PAGE BRIEF VIOLATED C R C 3.1113(D) AND SHE OBTAINED NO PERMISSION FOR AN OVER LENGTH BRIEF PURSUANT TO C R C 3.1113(E). THE COURT TREATS IT AS A LATE BRIEF PURSUANT TO C R C 3.1113 (G) AND ELECTS NOT TO CONSIDER IT PURSUANT TO C R C 3.1300. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WAS FILED LATE AND WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED. EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO CONSIDER THE PARTIES PAPERS, THE MOTION WOULD BE DENIED. FIRST BECAUSE IT WAS FILED MORE THAN 60 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND SECOND BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT WHICH IS BASED ON BREACH OF REPAYMENT OBLIGATIONS DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED CONDUCT FOR PURPOSES OF C C P 425.16, NOTWITHSTANDING WHATEVER PLAINTIFF'S MOTIVE MAY HAVE BEEN IN BRINGING THE SUIT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT LIGIA MELENDEZ' Motion To Strike Slapp Suit And For Sanctions is DENIED. DEFENDANT'S 23 PAGE BRIEF VIOLATED C R C 3.1113(D) AND SHE OBTAINED NO PERMISSION FOR AN OVER LENGTH BRIEF PURSUANT TO C R C 3.1113(E). THE COURT TREATS IT AS A LATE BRIEF PURSUANT TO C R C 3.1113 (G) AND ELECTS NOT TO CONSIDER IT PURSUANT TO C R C 3.1300. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WAS FILED LATE AND WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED. EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO CONSIDER THE PARTIES PAPERS, THE MOTION WOULD BE DENIED. FIRST BECAUSE IT WAS FILED MORE THAN 60 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND SECOND BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT WHICH IS BASED ON BREACH OF REPAYMENT OBLIGATIONS DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED CONDUCT FOR PURPOSES OF C C P 425.16, NOTWITHSTANDING WHATEVER PLAINTIFF'S MOTIVE MAY HAVE BEEN IN BRINGING THE SUIT. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/LB
